symptoms.symptom_name: Acute profound weakness, bedbound for 24 hours
symptoms.onset: acute crash today after two weeks of decline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Functional collapse tracks the timeline of medication and food rationing.
Profound weakness is consistent with circulatory compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete cessation of essential daily medication (halved x2 weeks, now out)
symptoms.duration: 14 days of halving, now fully ceased

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: If the essential medication is a corticosteroid, abrupt cessation precipitates adrenal crisis.
The stopped medication is the direct precipitant named in the history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe caloric restriction / undernutrition with recent weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged starvation predisposes to electrolyte derangement and refeeding syndrome.
Reduced fluid and food intake contributes to hypovolaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic light-headedness and palpitations on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural light-headedness and reflex tachycardia are patient-reportable surrogates for hypotension.
Postural hypotension is a feature of adrenal insufficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: 72h sleep deprivation with shame, hopelessness and burdensomeness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Adjustment disorder with anxiety and depressed mood
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stressor-linked insomnia, shame and low mood define the adjustment-disorder substrate.
Hopelessness and burdensomeness are suicide-risk markers mandating a screen.
Objective hypotension — sealed clinician measurement; the AI can only infer this from orthostatic symptoms.
Compensatory tachycardia for hypotension/hypovolaemia — sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weak peripheral pulses and delayed capillary refill (3 seconds)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral hypoperfusion signs — obtainable only by hands-on examination, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever (36.5 C) and no localising infective source

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever or source lowers, but does not exclude, sepsis in an undernourished patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: Globally reduced motor strength (4/5) and cognitive sluggishness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global weakness and slowing from undernutrition and medication withdrawal — examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum electrolytes, glucose and cortisol / venous blood gas

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urgent bloods — U&E, glucose, cortisol, FBC, CRP; ECG
investigation_details.expected_result: Not yet obtained; needed to exclude adrenal crisis, electrolyte disturbance, arrhythmia and occult sepsis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Serious organic mimics remain unexcluded; the acute decompensation cannot be safely characterised remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person bloods are required to confirm or exclude these time-critical mimics.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Circulatory-compromise cluster after medication and food rationing
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these indicate genuine circulatory compromise requiring urgent in-person vitals and bloods — the core reason this case cannot be safely managed by telehealth reassurance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person vital signs (BP, HR, postural drop, temperature)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective vitals cannot be obtained by the AI and are the first step in characterising the decompensation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent bloods (U&E, glucose, cortisol, FBC, CRP) and ECG
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to exclude adrenal crisis, electrolyte disturbance/arrhythmia and occult sepsis.
